channel.title: Comments for page "Excerpts from PHYSICS Division Threat Entity Database"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Excerpts from PHYSICS Division Threat Entity Database"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've reverted Cetus back to my original definition of "Sea monster" (it had previously been "Massive aquatic lifeform"). This was once objected to under the grounds of being unofficial sounding, but this list is *not* official sounding: one of the entries is "Atypical Angel". In comparison to that definition, sea monster fits in fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you read through the forum posts, you'd find there's a long-standing policy of not posting new codewords until they're used in articles. If you'd like to include your ideas, please write articles containing them first.

Kept Poe and Godwin, because I like them and think those hubs deserve a lot of interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added "Occam" (as in, Occam's Razor) in the Extranormal category which describes anomalies that alter with probabilities and casuality.

Edited as I did not completely check, instead I added "Godwin" (for Francis Godwin, an Elizabethan science fiction author known for "The Man in the Moone, or a Discourse of a Voyage thither, by Domingo Gonsales") and "Poe" (for Edgar Allan Poe, a poet known for his tales involving mystery and horror in a macabre setting) to denote the Three Moons Initiative and Parawatch respectively.

Added Rothko, Newton, Ratha, Void, Moai, Tesseract, and Amityville.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverted this change since I use it in UTE-1919-Discofather.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed Type Beige for the time being, as it a) doesn’t fit with the Humanoid section very well and b) hasn’t appeared in any articles yet. May add it back in some form if I can contact the author and iron out the problems. Also may remove other terms that haven’t been used yet as well, as to not clutter up the page (sorry for starting that trend).

item.title: Possible Codeword Additions
item.content:encoded:
Here's a list of GOC codewords that I've thought of with their origins and what they're used for (I'll add more when I think of them):
Codeword: Used For: Origin:
Geist Threat Entities related to Nobody. I thought of using it as a "ghost" to describe Nobody.
Schrödinger Threat Entities that are paradoxical in nature. This is supposed to come from Schrödinger's cat.
Grimm Threat Entities related to faes, fairies, etc. The name comes from Grimms' Fairy Tales.

Though, I don't know if these words should be added and (if so) where.

item.title: Question:
item.content:encoded: Why is there no Deer College codename? Is it because it is jist a school, or is it because its in Three Portlands?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since there is more content about SAPPHIRE today, I've added its codeword, "Dawkins", after suggesting the idea to Gémini, the creator of the GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A little bit puzzled of the Humanoid. What are Type White and Type Beige for?

And I saw LTE-4269-Disco-Purple and UTE-7269-Disco-Purple in UTE-1919-Discofather, so do this means Type Purple? Should this be added in this page?

Not very clear about what can be added in this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added the designation "-dianetic" for non-GoI groups formed directly because of an entity. (An example would be a small cult worshipping a KTE)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Write up a tale with the term, and if the tale sticks around, add it to this page. That's sort of the consensus on the best way to go about things. Which is at least half of the reason I wrote KTE-Kapala-Mendes. I just love my codeword porn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw suggestions for Type Pink, which is for mutant. I am considering using it but wouldn't that fall under Type Yellow? It says for other types of enhanced humans while listing some anomalous attributes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for notifying me.

I forgot to remove it. It slipped off my mind due to life matters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If an object isn't on the mainsite, an entry shouldn't be added?

Yes, since to allow otherwise kind of invites everyone to just throw low-quality OCs and whatnot onto the hub.

Staff purview typically results in entries being removed if the only feature they have is on this page and they're not used at all elsewhere on the site.

If you do not remove the entry, staff will do so momentarily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh…

I should remove it.

If an object isn't on the mainsite, an entry shouldn't be added?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've still got the "on going" typo, and misuse of the word "termination" (you probably want "termination efforts" or "acquisition for termination" or the like).

Also, is this Demon added currently used in any mainsite works? If not, it seems a little odd to put in prematurely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've added a Demon in the database.

EDIT: And changed 'liquidation' to 'termination' IN my own entry, and some of the grammar and typos in my entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just added Disco as the codeword for the Chicago Spectre, after suggesting the idea to ARD, creator of the group.

Notably: the Chicago Spirit itself does not have a codeword, as it never existed concurrently with the Global Occult Coalition. (Spirit is over in 1938, GOC formed in 1945.) It therefore would not have interacted with the GOC to an extent that would require a codeword.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have changed the definition of Cetus to something closer to my original definition, as I was the original individual to add it to the page and wrote the only document that uses it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could I add the link to KTE-4622-Velveteen "Hunting Mannequin" from this SCP document?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Modified 'Type Yellow' to include psychics, broadening the definition, in line with Kakroom's tale, Saudade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also changed 'Large-Scale Sea Monster' to the more official sounding 'Aquatic gigafauna,' for the Cetus code-word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Doc. Made an edit to the Schopenhauer codeword.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m currently asking about GOC stuff from other sites via the SCP-INT wiki’s discord server. I’ll update this post with anything I figure out with their help.

EDIT I: I've gotten a better idea of what and Schopenhauer and Goodrick mean by asking the author about them (On discord he's "Karcist Haimdall"). Schopenhauer is a reference to Arther Schopenhauer, and spedicicly his work "The World as Will and Representation/Die Welt als Wille und Vorstellung", and Goodrick is a based off of Nicholas Goodrick-Clarke, and his work "The Occult Roots of Nazism".

Schopenhauer is a designation for "living land", or as TV Tropes refers to it, "Genius Loci". How exactly we should say that I don't really know. I've edited Goodrick's definiton to something a tad more presise imo, as "non-humanoid occult threat" basically applies to everything in the non-humanoid category, as occult can be interpreted as everything supernatural, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added the GOC format documents from the other sites; added Schopenhauer and Gookrick from SCP-ES-014; removed Type Pink, given the lack of documents associated with that code word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, just found three GOC pages on the JA site. Links here, here, and here. Should I add these? Also, this, their own Japanese GOC branch hub.

EDIT: Also, this. Someone should go through the other sites and gather some of this stuff to put on the hub page.

Edit: Found an SCP on the Spanish site with a GOC document in it, here. It had the following codewords:
Schopenhauer: 'Entities associated with abnormal geographic regions'
Cookrick: Non-humanoid occult threats.

Using this post to gather up GOC pages from the other branches, which I will likely post in a bit, unless anyone has objections.

From the French site: here. 'Berce' meaning Hogweed.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: Also, both Yellow and Pink are used in other articles. Several authors have different headcanon about them. You're being kind of presumptuous. If you look back through this discussion page, you'll see a trend- people don't make impulsive edits and additions to a page as important to as many people's canon as this one without having them reverted.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: I say make a link on this GOI page to the SCP-KO KTE-2683 entry. That way, people can tell that Type Yellow is being used in actual writing and not just added by someone who wants other people to write their stuff for them.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: Yes, I've made a thinkg using Type Yellow. and this article would be part of our own canon project.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
We were in coures of making (our own) canon, then realized that main wiki's GOC canon lacks some code we needed.

Write those canon pieces first, then add the codes in. Ideally, the codes would only be added once there stories that actually use the codes. Otherwise, it seems like there's no reason to have the codes if no one has used them.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Sorry, but this page is a part of canon, and it is used by other wikis as well, as English wiki plays leading role(i.e. canons of EN wiki are shared in other sites usually).

…and? There are plenty of elements from our sister sites that don't show up on the main wiki. We don't constantly edit our task force page for every team each other site generates - nor do our content creators abroad need to rigidly adhere to Information pages on this site in order to produce anything.

I don't think I deserve such censure.

Does that stifle creativity? Is it censure? I'd wager that it doesn't, and it isn't, because people are perfectly capable of generating their own content without meddling around on collaborative pages that have no staff oversight. How many people on our sister sites can't function if Word of EN wiki isn't sacrosanct with any number of their works-in-progress?

We (at least, I) thought the canon of main wiki take priority, so adding entry on main wiki canon should be done first.

This is what's backwards here. The creation of articles is what drives and shifts canon.

What if someone who is actually producing content wants to use a certain color or name for something they're working on? Why should they have to adhere to something someone completely unrelated to the main page added on a whim? Why should their creativity be stifled by risking downvotes or complaints because "well, violet means they're psychic, not telepathic. Downvote."

By doing this, you're essentially dictating what other people get to use without actually doing any of the heavy lifting yourself. It's selfish. It's unfair. It's lazy.

Long post collapsed. ~Zyn

So, in a nutshell, not visible output yet.

This…is exactly what the problem is. What happens when whatever story you're working on is deleted? Falls to oblivion? What happens if it's never posted to begin with? Then all we're left with is an ever-expanding glut of neat words stuck there by people who don't have anything substantial to attach to it.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
I mean to say that you can link to the Korean articles that use the codenames you propose here, so that one can see that you are not merely dropping ideas for the sake of it.

I hold no stake in this canon/GoI hub, but I think it would be a suitable move to show your sincerity in your expansion to the lore of the canon/GoI.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
We were in coures of making (our own) canon, then realized that main wiki's GOC canon lacks some code we needed. We (at least, I) thought the canon of main wiki take priority, so adding entry on main wiki canon should be done first.

So, in a nutshell, not visible output yet. But thanks for your advice, and I'll bear it in mind.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: In that case, I suppose it is good practice to cite evidence and instances a given codename is used (so long it is something situated in the Foundation setting). I did that when I added the 'Ford' codename to refer to CotBG.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: Sorry, but this page is a part of canon, and it is used by other wikis as well, as English wiki plays leading role(i.e. canons of EN wiki are shared in other sites usually). Essentially I'm SCP-KO's user, and we use this canon for our own contents creation. Is it so bad to participate in modifying main wiki's canon in order to our own usage? I don't think I deserve such censure.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: I think a better idea than people constantly using this page as a dump for cool codewords would be for more people to actually create some content for the group that can stand on its own.

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
I boldly added Yellow and Pink. Problem is Type Orange (my suggestion) or Indigo (Greenwolf's).

If many think Indigo is better, I would yield. Plz post your opinions, please!

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
And other suggestions:

  • the person with extraordinary lifespan (immortal or perennial youth) — Type Pink (from western amaranth and asian peach)
  • shapeshifter — Type Yellow

item.title: Re: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded: I would offer up the alternate suggestion of Type Indigo for prophets, prescients, and seers.

item.title: Suggestion for new color type
item.content:encoded:
I think a prophecy ability can be separate category. A prophecy which is anomally accurate. For example, SCP-657.

How about Type Orange?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding 'Burrhus' and 'Faraday'- behavioral alteration and electrical hazard, respectively, both (one assumes) derived from the names of scientists- BF Skinner and Michael Faraday- and both from Dr0Shadow's Roc Series Deicide Drone page. Also thinking of adding some stuff on the main GOC hub about the member groups mentioned on that page- if the author doesn't want to do so himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I call everybody 'boss.' Picked it up from my dad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(boss?)

No problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, thanks, boss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good policy. Generally a good idea to be conservative with other people's work. Otherwise, I'd have dumped my metric fuck-load of headcanon all over the GOC.

Also, I've read that story. No idea how that slipped my mind. Thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Fair enough.
  2. Technically there are two others in additon to that, plus the two fractions of post-WWII IJAMEA (the latter could be argued that they should be considered separate orgs). For now I think its safe to leave it how it is until those become more visible on this site.
  3. Blit (I figured out) comes from a short story of the same name and its sequels, centering around images that we would call cognitohazards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alphabetized Meglo's previous additions, and added the Oneiroi collectives under the political section (its how the foundation seams to think of them, so I would think the coalition would share a similar view on the matter), under the name of "Flanders" (as suggested by Roget, based on Flanders Fields, the WWI battlefields). The code-phrase motif is types of dreams, Fever (due to the unpleasant surreal aspects), Lucid (for the coherency shared with the subject matter and the real phenomena), and Rapture (after the thematic similarity I perceived between both).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The morph thing would probably be too complicated, if a group was prone to multiple reinventions, and also a little self-referential for a bunch of hard-headed government types. I think they'd just scrub the old classification, and come up with a new code-word for the new organization.

For Japan, there was also the pre-IJAMEA group, the Bureau of Onmyo, though I think that was only ever referenced on the IJAMEA hub page, and maybe one skip. Not sure if it'd qualify for a code-word.

For the Blit code-word (which I guess was derived from a computer science term? I dunno) I'd say 'cognitive' or 'neurocognitive' or 'cognitive or perceptual.' Cognition hazard is correct, but not very slick.

Good batch of ideas, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a link to KTE-2013-Kapala-Mendes, and the code-words from same, in the appropriate tabs, since it seems to be sticking around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Additions:

Threat Warning

  • Blit (seen in KTE-5321-Blit-Gray [or Grey-Blit] "Eyeblight," on the Humanoid special circumstances page): Cognition Hazard.

Political

  • Aurora: State of Japan (with names coming after atmospheric phenomena)
    • Aurora Glory: [insert actual Japanese name for them]/ Imperial Japanese Anomalous Matters Examination Agency (IJAMEA), defunct.

Suggestion: We create a "[XXX]morph" sub-category for orgs and politics, so that organizations created by the liquidation of an org (like Prometheus Labs and IJAMEA) are represented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Couldn't find Monetary when I was look for a word meaning related/of money. Should of looked harder.

Alexandria will be moving to artifacts for now, although I think it actual may refer to the library itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alexandria probably shouldn't be in a category all on its own. I'd suggest moving it to the Artifact section.

Regarding Flint: 'Monetary' would seem a good fit. Plutarchical is both not a word, and if broke down, means 'like or of a plutocrat.'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Revison to the ORIA's codeword:

  • "Andromeda" Becomes the codeword for Iran/Persia in the political section.
  • ORIA becomes "Andromeda Sesame" (Sesame after Ali Baba and the Forty Thieves).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Additions taken from around the site:

Threat Warning

  • Pygmalion (from KTE-0937-Velveteen's file): Parathreat Creation.

Artifact.

  • Flint (from Conspiracy, Part III): plutarchical object (best word I could find. If someone has something better please replace this one).

Locations (new category)

  • Alexandria (from Angle of Attack): Bibliographical in nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Additions to Organizational/Political

  • Carcinoma (The Chaos Insurgency)
  • Renegade Latrans (Unusual Incidents Unit, Federal Beurua of Investigation)
  • Retcon (Daevites)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: General commonsense from the Chat says Carcinoma. Going with the majority here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the GOC knew about the actual origins of the Insurgency, I'd say "glioma", but as they don't I'd have to go with…Neoplasm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the codeword for the UIU (though I'm unaware of any link between the Bowe commission and that group in any works on the site). 'Cancer' is a bit on the nose, though. Maybe 'Carcinoma,' or 'Neoplasm'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cancer, as in a harmful growth of rapidly dividing cells. As in Lung and Prostate. Kinda surprised you didn't get that.

Renegade Latrans is a continuation of Renegade Lupus (For the bowe commission), Latrans being the scientific name for coyote (a scavenger, which fits the UIU's situation fairly well).

As for the others, I'll probably suggest them again when they do get onto the main site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure I get the reference for CI and UIU, and I'm not don't think the two GOI from our sister sites need to be added with so little content of theirs on the mainsite (the MC is only referenced once, briefly in a translated Tale). The Daevite identifier is pretty cool though.
